topic: Why airplanes can fly with one engine
desiredTakeaway: Modern twin-engine airplanes are designed to keep flying safely after one engine fails.

factPack.claim: Commercial twin-engine aircraft are certified to continue safe flight and landing after one engine fails.
Pilots train for engine-out procedures in simulators before they fly passengers.
A remaining engine provides thrust while the wings still generate lift, so the airplane does not suddenly fall.
Route planning rules account for how far a plane can fly to an alternate airport on one engine.

sourceNotes: Keep the explanation calm and confidence-building.
Avoid implying that engine failure is routine.
bannedClaims: Any statement that all engine failures are harmless.
